verdict.answer_first: A buries it behind “No worries”; B’s first line says “plan for the most likely case — Postgres,” but the actionable recommendation appears under “Use Postgres's built-in full-text search.”
verdict.decisiveness: B commits to one path: “Use Postgres's built-in full-text search”; A splits into “Your branch (pick by DB)” and surveys options.
verdict.fabrication: B invents context: “Your global context leans heavily on Aurora PostgreSQL”; both give unverified latency claims, A “under ~50ms,” B “single-digit milliseconds.”
verdict.actionability: B is easier to execute because it gives concrete SQL migrations and query code; A stays mostly framework-level with DB branches.
